name: Snapper
description: Known for its vibrant color, snapper is a popular fish caught in Zamboanga’s waters.
location: Zamboanga del Norte, Zamboanga del Sur

name: Spanner Crab
description: A rare, deep-sea crab species that’s highly prized in local cuisine, especially in Zamboanga City.

name: Anchovy
description: Small but economically important, dilis is often dried and used as a condiment or side dish.

name: Sea Grapes
description: A type of edible green algae used in local salads and as a side dish in coastal communities.

name: Batfish
description: Characterized by its disc-like shape and long fins, this fish is common in Zamboanga’s coral reefs.

name: Goby
description: Small, often colorful fish found in the rocky tidal zones and reefs of Zamboanga’s coastal areas.

name: Tabios
description: The world’s smallest commercially harvested fish, endemic to the Philippines.

name: Giant Tiger Prawn
description: A key aquaculture species grown in the mangrove areas of Zamboanga.

name: Sardine
description: Small oily fish found in large schools, widely used in the production of canned sardines, especially in Zamboanga City.

name: Spanish Mackerel
description: A fast-swimming predatory fish that is economically significant in Zamboanga’s fishing industry.
